parsed.summary: The v0.4.0→v0.4.6 cumulative diff introduces ~6600 lines across 84 files including two new core reliability modules (retry.mjs, bigmodel-errors.mjs) and significant modifications to the client, state management, and job-tracking layers. Three structural concerns block confidence: (1) no UAT evidence exists for v0.4.6—the release that added the most safety-critical code paths (retry/backoff + error dispatch); (2) process.mjs was deleted with no clear evidence all consumers were migrated; (3) the commit history shows 5 successive hotfix bundles (C→F) followed by 6 patch releases, a pattern consistent with residual instability. The cumulative blast radius is too large to ship without targeted validation of the v0.4.6-era code paths.

findings.title: No UAT evidence for v0.4.6 retry/backoff and error-dispatch features
findings.body: UAT report directories exist for v0.4.4 and v0.4.5 but not v0.4.6. The v0.4.6 release (commit e0a9ba8) introduced 'BigModel error dispatch + auto retry/backoff'—the most safety-critical additions in the entire cumulative diff (retry.mjs at 203 lines, bigmodel-errors.mjs at 168 lines, glm-client.mjs with 155 lines changed). These modules control how transient API failures are classified, retried, and surfaced. Without end-to-end UAT validation under real failure conditions (rate-limiting, timeouts, partial responses), the retry logic could silently swallow errors, retry non-idempotent operations, or exhaust backoff budgets on cascading failures. The existing retry.test.mjs (352 lines) likely tests the module in isolation; integration with glm-client.mjs and tracked-jobs.mjs under realistic failure is unverified.
findings.recommendation: Create a v0.4.6 UAT suite that exercises: (a) API rate-limit responses triggering backoff and eventual success, (b) persistent 5xx exhaustion hitting max-retry ceiling with correct error propagation, (c) concurrent job submissions that hit retry paths simultaneously, (d) mid-retry interruption and recovery. Do not ship v0.4.7 until this evidence exists.

findings.title: process.mjs deleted without visible migration audit
findings.body: scripts/lib/process.mjs (67 lines removed per diff stat) was deleted in the cumulative change. This file likely exported process-management utilities consumed by other modules. The diff stat shows modifications to glm-companion.mjs, glm-client.mjs, render.mjs, and other lib files but the repository context does not include evidence that all import sites were updated. A dangling `import ... from './process.mjs'` or `from './lib/process.mjs'` would produce a runtime ModuleNotFoundError that only manifests when the specific code path executes—potentially not during happy-path testing.
findings.recommendation: Run `grep -r "process\.mjs" scripts/` across the codebase to confirm zero remaining import references. Add an explicit import-existence check to the CI gate (scripts/check-imports.mjs already exists—verify it covers dynamic imports and re-exports). If any consumer was missed, restore the module or migrate the consumer.

findings.title: Hotfix-bundle commit pattern suggests residual defects in core modules
findings.body: The commit log shows five successive hotfix bundles (C, D3+, E+, F) plus six patch releases (v0.4.1 through v0.4.6), with Bundle F described as 'ReferenceError + full thread/resume scaffolding purge.' A ReferenceError reaching production suggests insufficient import/reference validation and potentially other unresolved references. The cumulative effect is that core modules (state.mjs, job-control.mjs, tracked-jobs.mjs, glm-client.mjs) have been modified in each round, increasing the risk that invariants established in earlier bundles were silently broken by later ones. No integration test file appears in the tests/ directory that validates the full companion lifecycle end-to-end.
findings.recommendation: Add a single integration test that exercises the full lifecycle: companion init → job submission → API call (mocked failure) → retry/backoff → eventual success or exhaustion → state persistence → status query. This would catch class-level regressions that per-module unit tests miss. Prioritize testing state.mjs invariants (atomic writes, no partial state) since every hotfix bundle touched it.

findings.title: tracked-jobs.mjs modifications lack race-condition evidence
findings.body: scripts/lib/tracked-jobs.mjs (78 lines changed) manages job tracking state. The diff stat does not include any concurrency-oriented test file (no test file with 'concurrent', 'race', or 'parallel' in its name). If job tracking uses file-based state (likely given fs.mjs changes), concurrent job submissions could interleave reads and writes, corrupting the job registry. The v0.4.5 UAT scenario-G and scenario-I files are only 21 lines each (minimal), and the v0.4.5 run-uat.sh at 335 lines likely does not exercise concurrent job submission.
findings.recommendation: Add a test that spawns two concurrent job-registration operations against the same state file and verifies the resulting state is consistent (no lost jobs, no corrupted JSON). If file-based locking is missing, add it before v0.4.7 ships. Document the concurrency model in tracked-jobs.mjs header comments.

findings.title: configure-gitea-protection.sh excluded from path-leak guard but contains external references
findings.body: commit 76bdbd7 explicitly excludes configure-gitea-protection.sh from the path-leak guard ('Bundle G fixup — exclude ... configure-gitea-protection.sh from path-leak guard'). This script (126 lines) likely contains internal hostnames, URLs, or paths that the guard is designed to catch. The exclusion may be necessary for functionality but creates a maintenance risk: future edits could introduce leaks that the CI will not detect.
findings.recommendation: Add a line-level allowlist to the path-leak guard for the specific patterns this script needs, rather than blanket-excluding the entire file. Add a comment in the script header documenting why it contains external references and what patterns are expected.

next_steps: Create and execute a v0.4.6 UAT suite covering retry/backoff failure scenarios before any v0.4.7 work proceeds.
Run `grep -r 'process\.mjs' scripts/` to confirm the deleted module has no remaining consumers.
Add a full-lifecycle integration test (init → submit → fail → retry → resolve → status) to tests/.
Add a concurrent-job registration test for tracked-jobs.mjs to validate file-based state integrity under parallel writes.
Re-evaluate the configure-gitea-protection.sh CI exclusion and replace with a targeted allowlist.
